steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows "preclude claims of the event's ______." Statistical analysis confirmed the signal at over 99% confidence, so whatever the blank names is what that analysis ruled out. The correct word must describe what high confidence eliminates — some form of doubt or unclear status about the detection.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — discretion: Means careful judgment or freedom to decide — unrelated to scientific uncertainty about a detection. ✗
  • Choice B — ambiguity: Means lack of clarity or having multiple interpretations. A 99%+ confidence level directly eliminates ambiguity about whether the signal was real. ✓
  • Choice C — probability: Inverts the logic — the analysis established high probability; it didn't preclude probability. ✗
  • Choice D — inconspicuousness: Means being hard to notice or faint. Skeptics worried about faintness, but the stated confidence level addresses how certain the researchers were that the signal was not noise, not how faint the signal was. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
